request.question: What 2012 South Korean action thriller film was turned into the 2014 Indian-Malay thriller Angels?

title: The Villainess
Confession of Murder
Tony Scott
No Tears for the Dead
The Chaser (film)
Tube (2003 film)
Ek Villain
A Company Man
Angels (2014 film)
Hwayi: A Monster Boy
sentences: The Villainess is a 2017 South Korean action thriller film directed by Jung Byung-gil, starring Kim Ok-bin.
The film had its world premiere at the 70th Cannes Film Festival in May 2017.
Confession of Murder (; lit.
"I am the Murderer") is a 2012 South Korean action thriller film directed by Jung Byung-gil, starring Jung Jae-young and Park Si-hoo.

It is about a police officer who is haunted for failing to capture a serial killer 15 years back, and returns to the case after a novelist publishes the book "I am the Murderer", claiming responsibility for the crimes.
Anthony David Leighton Scott (21 June 1944 – 19 August 2012) was an English film director and producer.

His films come from a broad range of genres, including the action drama "Top Gun" (1986), action comedy "Beverly Hills Cop II" (1987), auto racing film "Days of Thunder" (1990), action comedy "The Last Boy Scout" (1991), romantic dark comedy crime film "True Romance" (1993), submarine action film "Crimson Tide" (1995), psychological thriller "The Fan" (1996), spy thriller "Enemy of the State" (1998), spy film "Spy Game" (2001), action thriller "Man on Fire" (2004), sci-fi action thriller "Déjà Vu" (2006), thriller "The Taking of Pelham 123" (2009), and the action thriller "Unstoppable" (2010).

No Tears for the Dead (; literally Crying Man) is a 2014 South Korean action thriller film written and directed by Lee Jeong-beom, about a professional hitman (Jang Dong-gun) who is conflicted about killing his last target (Kim Min-hee).
The Chaser () is a 2008 South Korean action thriller film starring Kim Yoon-seok and Ha Jung-woo.
It was directed by Na Hong-jin in his directorial debut.
Inspired by real-life Korean serial killer Yoo Young-chul, the film was shot on location around Mangwon-dong in the Mapo District, Seoul.
Tube () is a 2003 South Korean action thriller film directed by Beak Woon-hak (or Baek Woon-hak).
The film features police officer Jay (Kim Suk-hoon) who is a subway police officer who spends his days reminiscing over his lost lover.
The pickpocket Kay (Bae Doona) becomes infatuated with Jay and tips him off about the government assassin Bishop (Park Sang-min) hijacking a subway car.
Both Jay and Kay find themselves in the target car when the Bishop makes his move.
Ek Villain (English: "One Villain") is a 2014 Indian romantic thriller directed by Mohit Suri.
The film stars Sidharth Malhotra, Shraddha Kapoor and Riteish Deshmukh in lead roles, and tells the story of a hardened criminal whose terminally ill wife is murdered by a serial killer.
The film was produced by Ekta Kapoor and Shobha Kapoor under Balaji Motion Pictures and ALT Entertainment.
The film is an unofficial remake of the 2010 South Korean action thriller film "I Saw the Devil".
Upon its release on 27 June 2014, the film received mixed to positive reviews from the critics, and was a commercial success.
A Company Man () is a 2012 South Korean action thriller film starring So Ji-sub, Lee Mi-yeon, Kwak Do-won and Kim Dong-jun.
It is about a hitman who find himself targeted by his ex-employers after he falls in love with a single mother and quits his job.
Angels is a 2014 Indian Malayalam social thriller film and the directorial debut of Jean Markose.
The film stars an ensemble cast consisting of Indrajith, Asha Sarath, Joy Mathew, Prem Prakash, Parvathy Menon, Baiju and Dinesh Panicker.
Story and screenplay were written by Jean Markose and Toni Tomy, dialogues were co-written by Shabu Kilithatil.
It is a rip-off from the South Korean film "Confession of Murder" (2012).
This film is a Moderated Success and Hit.

Hwayi: A Monster Boy () is a 2013 South Korean action thriller film about a 16-year-old boy of the same name (played by Yeo Jin-goo) who is raised by five criminal fathers to become the perfect assassin.
It takes pulling the trigger to discover his true identity after he realizes the mystery surrounding his past and his fate.
It was the highly anticipated second feature film by director Jang Joon-hwan, a decade after his 2003 cult favorite sci-fi comedy/thriller "Save the Green Planet!

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: What 2012 South Korean action thriller film was turned into the 2014 Indian-Malay thriller Angels?
hop1_docs: Angels (2014 film) | Angels is a 2014 Indian Malayalam social thriller film and the directorial debut of Jean Markose.  The film stars an ensemble cast consisting of Indrajith, Asha Sarath, Joy Mathew, Prem Prakash, Parvathy Menon, Baiju and Dinesh Panicker.  Story and screenplay were written by Jean Markose and Toni Tomy, dialogues were co-written by Shabu Kilithatil.  It is a rip-off from the South Korean film "Confession of Murder" (2012).  This film is a Moderated Success and Hit.

Ek Villain | Ek Villain (English: "One Villain") is a 2014 Indian romantic thriller directed by Mohit Suri.  The film stars Sidharth Malhotra, Shraddha Kapoor and Riteish Deshmukh in lead roles, and tells the story of a hardened criminal whose terminally ill wife is murdered by a serial killer.  The film was produced by Ekta Kapoor and Shobha Kapoor under Balaji Motion Pictures and ALT Entertainment.  The film is an unofficial remake of the 2010 South Korean action thriller film "I Saw the Devil".  Upon its release on 27 June 2014, the film received mixed to positive reviews from the critics, and was a commercial success.

Holiday: A Soldier Is Never Off Duty | Holiday: A Soldier Is Never Off Duty is a 2014 Indian action thriller film written and directed by A. R. Murugadoss, and produced by Vipul Shah.  It features Akshay Kumar and Sonakshi Sinha in the lead roles, along with Farhad as the antagonist in his Bollywood debut and Govinda in a special appearance.  It is a remake of Murugadoss' own acclaimed Tamil film "Thuppakki" (2012).  The film released on 6 June 2014, becoming the biggest net grosser of the first half of 2014.  The movie was reported to be one of the best thrillers to come out of Bollywood.  It was also reported that Akshay Kumar's career took a new turn in terms of story selection and box office success with this movie.  Akshay Kumar was nominated for Filmfare Award for Best Actor at 60th Filmfare Awards.

Godfather (2012 film) | Godfather is a 2012 Indian Kannada Language action thriller musical film directed by cinematographer-turned- director Sethu Sriram starring Upendra and Soundarya Jayamala in the lead roles.  Catherine Tresa also plays a prominent role and Bhumika Chawla makes a special appearance.

Adah Sharma | Adah Sharma an Indian film actress who mainly appears in Hindi and Telugu language films.  Sharma, after finishing her schooling, made her acting debut with a leading role in the 2008 Hindi language horror film "1920", a box office success.  Her portrayal of a possessed woman in the film was critically praised and earned her the Filmfare Award for Best Female Debut nomination.  After the release of her critically and commercially successful romantic comedy film "Hasee Toh Phasee" (2014), she ventured into the South Indian film industries, where her first six films—five in Telugu language—the romantic thriller "Heart Attack" (2014), the drama "S/O Satyamurthy" (2015), the action comedy "Subramanyam for Sale" (2015), the romance "Garam" (2016) and the thriller "Kshanam" (2016) and one in Kannada— the action thriller "Rana Vikrama" (2015), achieved commercial success and garnered her critical appreciation for her performances.

A Hard Day | A Hard Day (; lit.  "Take It to the End") is a 2014 South Korean crime action thriller film written and directed by Kim Seong-hun, and starring Lee Sun-kyun and Cho Jin-woong.  It was selected to compete in the Directors' Fortnight section of the 2014 Cannes Film Festival.

Broken (2014 film) | Broken (; lit.  "Hovering Blade" or "Roving Edge") is a 2014 South Korean revenge action thriller film about a father (played by Jung Jae-young) who becomes a fugitive as he hunts down the two men responsible for his daughter's rape and murder.
hop1_titles: Holiday: A Soldier Is Never Off Duty

response.hop2_query: Confession of Murder 2012 South Korean film plot genre release
hop2_docs: Confession of Murder | Confession of Murder (; lit.  "I am the Murderer") is a 2012 South Korean action thriller film directed by Jung Byung-gil, starring Jung Jae-young and Park Si-hoo.  It is about a police officer who is haunted for failing to capture a serial killer 15 years back, and returns to the case after a novelist publishes the book "I am the Murderer", claiming responsibility for the crimes.

Over My Dead Body (2012 film) | Over My Dead Body (; lit.  "Return of the Corpse") is a 2012 South Korean comic heist film, starring Lee Beom-soo, Ryoo Seung-bum and Kim Ok-bin.  The plot centers on a biotech researcher, a woman whose father was murdered, and a man attempting to commit insurance fraud whose lives get tied up in the case of a stolen semiconductor chip and a missing corpse.  Released on March 29, 2012, the film sold 985,178 tickets in total.

Angels (2014 film) | Angels is a 2014 Indian Malayalam social thriller film and the directorial debut of Jean Markose.  The film stars an ensemble cast consisting of Indrajith, Asha Sarath, Joy Mathew, Prem Prakash, Parvathy Menon, Baiju and Dinesh Panicker.  Story and screenplay were written by Jean Markose and Toni Tomy, dialogues were co-written by Shabu Kilithatil.  It is a rip-off from the South Korean film "Confession of Murder" (2012).  This film is a Moderated Success and Hit.

Park Hae-il | Park Hae-il (born January 26, 1977) is a South Korean actor.  He began his acting career in theater, but soon gained the film industry's attention in 2003 with "Jealousy Is My Middle Name" and "Memories of Murder".  Park's film career took off, with leading roles in films of diverse genres, including relationship drama "Rules of Dating" (2005), horror mystery "Paradise Murdered" (2007), and crime thriller "Moss" (2010).  More recently, Park received Best Actor honors for his performance in the period action film "War of the Arrows", which was the highest-grossing Korean film of 2011.  He also received critical acclaim for his role as an aging poet in "A Muse" (2012).

Murder 2 | Murder 2 is a 2011 Indian psychological horror-slasher film and the quasi-sequel to the 2004 film, "Murder".  It stars Emraan Hashmi, Jacqueline Fernandez and Prashant Narayanan and features Sulagna Panigrahi.  Directed by Mohit Suri and produced by Mukesh Bhatt, the film released on 8 July 2011.  The theatrical trailer of the film was revealed on 1 June 2011 and also in cinemas with "Ready".  It was the second film in a series of quasi-sequels released under the Bhatt Banner including "Raaz – The Mystery Continues", "Jannat 2", "Jism 2", "Raaz 3D" and "", each of which had nothing to do with their respective prequels, but somehow fell into the same genre following a similar story.  The film did well at the box office and was declared a "blockbuster" by Box Office India.  It is one of the highest grossing Bollywood films of 2011.  The movie is based on the 2008 South Korean movie "The Chaser"
List of South Korean films of 2012 | This is a list of South Korean films released in 2012.

Park Si-hoo | Park Si-hoo (born Park Pyeong-ho on April 3, 1978) is a South Korean actor.  He began his entertainment career as an underwear model and stage actor, then made his official television debut in 2005.  After several years in supporting roles, Park rose to fame in 2010 with the popular romantic comedy series "Queen of Reversals" and "Prosecutor Princess".  This was followed by leading roles in period drama "The Princess' Man" (2011) and thriller film "Confession of Murder" (2012).
